qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via api_base parameter in LLM request body bypasses security check when api_key is also provided
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets LiteLLM completion endpoints and an api_base request-body safety bypass in is_request_body_safe/check_complete_credentials. The advisory targets the /config/update endpoint missing proxy_admin authorization, enabling authenticated non-admin proxy configuration and environment-variable changes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a privilege escalation caused by /config/update not enforcing proxy_admin authorization; the only final qualifying finding is a separate SSRF through api_base on completion endpoints.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-53mr-6c8q-9789 describes LiteLLM /config/update lacking admin role authorization and being fixed by requiring proxy_admin in v1.83.0.

ssrf_via_api_base_param/vulnerability_report.json describes POST /v1/chat/completions and related completion endpoints, with api_base allowed when api_key is present due to is_request_body_safe/check_complete_credentials.
Session output.scanned and .judge/input_manifest.json list ssrf_via_api_base_param as the only final qualifying vulnerability folder.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding is present, so Tier1/Tier2/Tier3 do not apply and no PoC execution is permitted for this judgement.
